summary: Richard S. Sutton proposes a 'common model of the intelligent agent' that unifies concepts across AI, psychology, neuroscience, economics, and control theory. He argues that these disciplines have converged on a shared understanding of goal-directed decision making, which involves interactions with a world (input, output, goals) and internal components (perception, decision-making, evaluation, world models), and calls for a neutral terminology to bridge these fields.

principles.statement: Multiple diverse disciplines have converged on a substantive common model of the intelligent agent.
principles.rationale: Fields like psychology, AI, economics, control theory, and neuroscience all share an interest in goal-directed decision making over time and refer to essentially the same ideas, despite using different names.
principles.source_quote: It is time to recognize and build on the convergence of multiple diverse disciplines on a substantive common model of the intelligent agent.

principles.statement: A foundational model of an intelligent agent must be entirely domain-general.
principles.rationale: To serve as a common model across disciplines, it must abstract away from the specifics of any particular implementation or environment.
principles.source_quote: The common model does not include anything specific to any organism, world, or application domain.

mental_models.name: The Common Model of the Intelligent Agent
mental_models.description: A universal perspective on decision makers divided into two main parts: the agent's interaction with its world (input, output, and a goal) and the agent's internal components (perception, decision-making, internal evaluation, and a world model).
mental_models.example: Used as a neutral framework to translate and unify concepts across psychology, AI, economics, control theory, and neuroscience.

signature_quotes.text: It is time to recognize and build on the convergence of multiple diverse disciplines on a substantive common model of the intelligent agent.
signature_quotes.context: Concluding thought in the abstract advocating for cross-disciplinary unification.

signature_quotes.text: The common model does not include anything specific to any organism, world, or application domain.
signature_quotes.context: Defining the domain-agnostic nature of the proposed common model.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on discipline-specific terminology for universal decision-making concepts.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It obscures the fact that different fields are referring to essentially the same ideas, creating artificial barriers between psychology, AI, economics, control theory, and neuroscience.
